frame_analyses.description: Title card: 'Cinch knot-laparoscopic sliding square knot' by Dr Rahul Jadhao
Laparoscopic view of Penrose drain model with two suture ends visible (green and blue instruments controlling loops)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase - drain positioned, instruments ready for knot demonstration

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers visible with suture forming initial loop configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Preparing for surgeon's knot (double throw) formation

frame_analyses.description: Pink/magenta and blue instruments wrapping suture in alternating pattern
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being formed with clear hand coordination

frame_analyses.description: First knot appears to be cinching, suture tension being applied
Instruments repositioning for second throw with hand exchange
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed - opposite hand now leading

frame_analyses.description: Single throw being formed with green and blue instruments in reverse configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw, hands properly alternated

frame_analyses.description: Second throw cinched, knot appearing secure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper tension application, knot formation complete
Smooth execution, proper hand alternation maintained

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot with suture ends visible, drain slit appears closed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All three throws complete, drain appears properly approximated

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/educational video, not a performance video. It contains a title card, narrator attribution to Dr Rahul Jadhao, and demonstrates proper knot-tying technique on a laparoscopic model. No trainee performance timing or objective scoring is applicable.
cannot_determine: Completion time - this is a demonstration, not a timed performance
Suture deviation measurements - camera angle and instructional framing do not permit precise mark identification
Drain status assessment - video shows technical demonstration rather than clinical task execution
technique_summary: Instructional video demonstrating proper intracorporeal sliding square knot technique on a laparoscopic trainer model. Shows correct sequence: surgeon's knot (double throw) followed by two alternating single throws with appropriate hand switching between throws. Drain approximation appears complete with no visible gaps.
strengths: Clear demonstration of surgeon's knot double-throw formation
Proper hand switching between all throws visibly demonstrated
Smooth instrument coordination and tension control
Clean knot formation with appropriate cinching technique

demonstrated_techniques: Surgeon's knot formation (double throw initial throw)
Proper suture tension management during knot cinching
Instrument coordination for intracorporeal knot tying
Sliding square knot mechanics on Penrose drain model
verbal_cues: Title indicates 'Cinch knot-laparoscopic sliding square knot' - emphasis on cinching mechanism
Attribution to Dr Rahul Jadhao suggests expert-level instruction
common_errors_addressed: Avoids loose or insecure throws by maintaining consistent tension
Prevents gaps in drain closure through proper knot positioning

notes: This is an expert demonstration video designed for educational purposes. It should NOT be scored against FLS performance rubrics as it is instructional content, not a trainee performance evaluation. The video effectively teaches proper technique for intracorporeal suturing with emphasis on the sliding square knot method.
